title: GSOC provides many-to-one notifications with sub-second latency via symmetric shared-key SOCs.
content:
## Argument

GSOC (Graffiti Single Owner Chunks) is a many-to-one notification primitive on Swarm. A signer key is mined offline so that its SOC address falls in the reader's neighborhood; that signer is then shared with all writers. Writers create SOC updates, and the chunks sync via ordinary neighborhood replication — no Trojan mining per message.

## Key properties

- **Direction:** many-to-one (multiple writers → one reader)
- **Encryption:** symmetric (shared signer key)
- **Latency:** **milliseconds** (no per-message mining)
- **Offline delivery:** yes — SOC persists in the neighborhood
- **Setup cost:** one-time signer mining (~10–30s per peer)
- **Receiver node:** full node only
- **Stamp type:** must be mutable

## API

```typescript
const signer = bee.gsocMine(targetOverlay, identifier, proximity)
await bee.gsocSend(batchId, signer, identifier, data)
const sub = bee.gsocSubscribe(address, identifier, { onMessage, onError, onClose })
```

## Role in Connect

GSOC is the low-latency rail for presence, doc-update pings, and collaboration signals — everything where the 2–10s PSS latency is unacceptable.
description: GSOC uses a mined signer whose SOC address lands in the reader's neighborhood; multiple writers update that SOC, syncing via neighborhood replication in under a second.
